steps.title: See the example relationship
steps.content: The first sentence gives a general finding: many mushroom species contain phenolic compounds such as cinnamic acid and catechin. The second sentence gives specific detected cases of those same compounds in named mushroom species.

steps.title: Reject cause and contrast
steps.content: The second sentence is not a result caused by the first, and it does not contrast with the first. It supports the general claim by naming particular compounds and the mushrooms where Ferreira found them.

steps.title: Pick the example marker
steps.content:
• A: signals cause and effect, which does not fit.
  • B: signals contrast, but the second sentence supports the first.
  • C: also signals contrast despite an obstacle.
  • D: introduces a specific example of the general finding.
The answer is D.
